title: Hot country, cold shoulder: Pew survey shows Trump's global approval tanking
content: Global opinion on Donald Trump is overwhelmingly negative, with a median of just 23% expressing confidence in his leadership. A new Pew survey reveals widespread disapproval of his handling of foreign policy, including Iran and trade tariffs. Confidence in the US as a reliable partner has plummeted, particularly in Canada and Europe, painting a stark contrast to Trump's claims of global admiration.

title: Sooryavanshi to get a separate changing room in England: ECB reveals why
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, recently called up to India’s national team, could become the country’s youngest international cricketer if he debuts against Ireland. During the England tour, he will use a separate changing room under ICC and ECB safeguarding rules for under-16 players. The ECB and BCCI are coordinating arrangements, while his parents are expected to accompany him throughout the tour.

title: Speaker Birla to decide on TMC, Sena (UBT) defections before Monsoon Session
content: According to sources, legal and constitutional experts attached to Parliament are currently examining the matter and are expected to provide their recommendations before a final decision is taken. Previous rulings by presiding officers in comparable situations are also being studied to ensure any decision is legally and constitutionally sound. Apart from the rebel factions of the TMC and Shiv Sena (UBT), the DMK has also sought a separate seating arrangement following the collapse of its long-standing alliance with the Congress.

title: Altman’s OpenAI just built its first chip, Jalapeño, and why Nvidia should be worried
content: OpenAI has unveiled Jalapeño, its first in-house AI chip, designed with Broadcom to run the models behind ChatGPT and Codex. Built for inference rather than training, the chip is pitched on efficiency, with OpenAI claiming far better performance per watt than off-the-shelf options. The move cuts its reliance on Nvidia and puts OpenAI alongside Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta in building custom silicon. First deployments are expected by year’s end, scaling through 2027.

title: Bill Gates says Jeffrey Epstein sought to blackmail him over extramarital affairs
content: Bill Gates revealed to US lawmakers that Jeffrey Epstein appeared to be contemplating blackmail over his extramarital affairs, citing "veiled" threats and draft emails. Gates, testifying before the House Oversight Committee, stated he was never blackmailed but acknowledged Epstein explored using personal information to maintain influence. He expressed regret over his association with the late sex offender, calling it a "grave error in judgment."

title: Can employees who never got appointment letters demand one now? What new labour code says
content: Employers must now issue appointment letters in a standardized format to all staff, including those in informal sectors, detailing job roles, pay, and benefits before work commences. This new mandate aims to prevent disputes and enhance clarity on service conditions. Existing employees also have three months to receive these letters. Non-compliance incurs significant financial penalties, with states expected to align their rules soon.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM swaps 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Why your dandruff keeps coming back? Understand science of recurring scalp conditions
content: You wash your hair regularly, and you may switch shampoos and even try home remedies, yet within days, the severe flakes return on your shoulders and come out in chunks. This cycle is what almost every one of us has been through, without even realising why it is happening, even if you are using an anti-dandruff shampoo. This is not a cleanliness problem but a problem with the ingredients of your shampoo and understanding of the science behind dandruff and scalp conditions, so you can finally do something lasting about it.

title: 'Suvendu supported me a lot': Mahua recalls 'very good personal ties' with Bengal CM
content: "In 2014, I was supposed to get a Lok Sabha ticket, but it didn't happen, and I cried all night. It was Suvendu who supported me. Later, he joined the BJP, and we don't speak anymore because he is in a different political party. But no one can forget our personal relationship, " Mahua Moitra said.

title: US scientists using pigeons to train medical AI tools for early stage cancer detection
content: Pigeons are inspiring a new wave of AI for medical diagnostics. Researchers are studying how these birds process visual information to develop AI that can spot subtle signs of diseases like cancer in medical scans, even when human experts miss them. This groundbreaking work aims to enhance early detection and reduce diagnostic errors, ultimately supporting doctors in their crucial work.

title: Xi's bold move: China to enforce its new ethnic unity law, globally
content: The Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law, which comes into force on July 1, seeks to strengthen what Beijing describes as a shared national identity among the country's ethnic groups. One provision extends potential legal liability to people and organisations outside China, an aspect that has drawn particular scrutiny. Rights advocates warn that the measure could provide fresh legal grounds for targeting overseas campaigners from communities such as the Uyghurs and Tibetans.

title: Watch: After 'successful curse' on Kane, Ghana witch doctor frees striker of spell
content: England's goalless World Cup draw with Ghana drew attention after Ghanaian witch doctor Nana Kwaku Bonsam claimed he had cursed Harry Kane and later lifted the hex following the striker's missed chance. The bizarre story sparked headlines, while pundits criticised England's flat performance and said they were fortunate to avoid defeat. Despite concerns, most believe Thomas Tuchel's side remains well placed ahead of its final group-stage match against Panama.

title: Chiefs, Raiders emerge as top trade destinations for Patriots WR Kayshon Boutte
content: New England Patriots wide receiver Kayshon Boutte continues to generate trade interest, with the Kansas City Chiefs and Las Vegas Raiders identified as potential landing spots. Reports indicate the Patriots are seeking a third-round pick for the 23-year-old, who has one year left on his rookie contract. Despite the speculation, Boutte has publicly expressed his desire to remain in New England, saying he hopes to spend the rest of his NFL career with the Patriots.

title: Josh Hokit stands by Michelle Obama remark despite White, Cormier backlash
content: Josh Hokit has defended his controversial Michelle Obama remark, saying he believed it was a compliment and an example of free speech. His explanation has not reduced criticism, with figures such as Dana White and Daniel Cormier strongly condemning the comment. Hokit, however, continues to stand by his words and has refused to apologize.

title: JJ Watt recalls dating struggles and his longtime crush on Jennifer Aniston
content: Former NFL star J.J. Watt once revealed his struggles with dating due to his high-profile career and the intense public scrutiny that could scare his to-be-girlfriend. He shared his desire for a partner who valued personality and could share simple joys. He emphasized mutual enhancement in a relationship. Interestingly, Watt also recounted a memorable encounter with his celebrity crush, Jennifer Aniston, describing it as a surreal, fan-like experience. He is currently happily married to Kealia Ohai, a former professional soccer player.

title: Mumbai local train murder: How argument over coach door led to stabbing; accused detained
content: Roshan Suvarna, 30, was apprehended in Panvel following a fatal stabbing on a local train. The incident occurred Tuesday night between Andheri and Borivali after an argument with passenger Mayank Lohar over closing a coach door during heavy rain. Lohar sustained a fatal abdominal wound and later died at Shatabdi Hospital. Police identified Suvarna through extensive CCTV analysis.

title: Summer travel hack: How to use your credit card to save big
content: When selecting a travel credit card, you should focus on the overall value proposition instead of the headline benefits. Ideally, core benefits on travel-centric cards should be evaluated.

title: Taylor Swift is reportedly struggling with the Blake Lively fallout
content: Taylor Swift and Blake Lively’s bond has only worsened over the last few months as rumors swirl about the actress being invited to the pop star’s wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen in the next few weeks. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce focus on their wedding, it seems the pop star has gone through a wave of emotions.

title: Ronaldo vs Messi at FIFA World Cup 2026: CR7 says Portugal vs Argentina clash would be 'top''
content: Cristiano Ronaldo ended a difficult week with two goals as Portugal beat Uzbekistan 5-0, topping Group K at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old became the first player to score in six men's World Cups. After the match, Ronaldo called a potential showdown with Lionel Messi "awesome," a clash that could materialise in the quarterfinals if Portugal secure top spot in their group against Colombia on Saturday.

title: ‘Suffered a heatstroke’: Italy rebuts Nato chief's claim on US flights supporting Iran strikes
content: Italy has refuted Nato chief Mark Rutte's claim that hundreds of US aircraft flew from Italian bases for operations against Iran. Rome insists only technical and logistical support was authorized, not combat missions. This clarification follows Rutte's remarks to Fox News, which sparked political backlash in Italy. The government maintains its actions align with constitutional and treaty obligations, refusing requests beyond approved limits.

title: Chetan didn't pay, Ketan didn't return: Guard recalls moments before man's murder at Pune fort
content: A security guard's account at Lohagad Fort has shed light on the chilling events leading to Ketan Agarwal's death. He observed Ketan arriving with Siya Goyal, followed by Chetan Chaudhary, who appeared to deliberately conceal his identity. Police allege Siya and Chetan, her lover and co-conspirator, then pushed Ketan into a gorge, with Siya later fabricating a story of an accidental fall. The couple is now in custody.

title: From his first goal at 17 to 'GOAT' at 39: How Messi built his legacy year by year
content: As Lionel Messi turns 39, his career remains a testament to extraordinary longevity. From scoring his first senior goal at 17 to surpassing 900 career goals, the Argentine has continued to thrive well beyond his prime years. With 351 goals after turning 30, World Cup glory, countless trophies and ongoing success with Inter Miami, Messi has proved that greatness has no age limit.

title: BJP claims IAF tender for 36 Rafales exposes Pak propaganda, says 'Rahul owes explanation'
content: The BJP has strongly criticized Rahul Gandhi, accusing him of echoing Pakistan's propaganda regarding the Rafale jets during Operation Sindoor. The party highlighted a recent IAF tender for support for all 36 Rafale jets as proof that Pakistan's claims of shooting them down were false. BJP leaders argue Gandhi questioned the Indian government instead of demanding evidence from Islamabad, thereby legitimizing an adversary's disinformation campaign.

title: 'You need to apologise': Ben Stokes breaks silence on nightclub controversy
content: England captain Ben Stokes said he apologised to teammates after he and Gus Atkinson missed the second Test for breaching the team's midnight curfew. Stokes admitted the nightclub controversy overshadowed the debuts of several young players and affected the squad. Cleared of serious misconduct but issued warnings, Stokes stressed accountability, denied any rift with coach Brendon McCullum, and said the team is now fully focused on winning the series decider against New Zealand.

title: Meet Animesh Kujur: From Bastar’s conflict zones to India’s fastest man
content: Animesh Kujur shattered the national men's 100m record with a 10.15-second sprint, securing his spot at the Glasgow Commonwealth Games. Hailing from Chhattisgarh's conflict-prone Bastar region, Kujur's journey from humble beginnings to athletic stardom is a testament to his dedication.  His remarkable achievements, including national records and international medals, highlight his ascent as India's fastest man.

title: Stock market: Which are top gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Stock markets experienced a significant rebound on Wednesday, with the Sensex and Nifty surging past key levels. This rally was fueled by a sharp decline in crude oil prices, robust buying in banking and IT sectors, and growing optimism surrounding a potential India-US trade agreement. The positive sentiment was further bolstered by easing geopolitical tensions and renewed foreign investor interest, leading to broad-based gains across various sectors.

title: China’s new ‘sin list’: Why Xi's anti-graft crusade is now policing golf, feng shui & luck
content: China's decade-long anti-corruption drive under Xi Jinping has transformed from tackling graft to enforcing political loyalty and ideological conformity. Party watchdogs now scrutinize officials' beliefs, associations, and leisure activities, extending beyond traditional bribery to include "political discipline" and "elegant corruption." This broadens the campaign's scope to ensure strict adherence to Beijing's directives and Xi's policy priorities across the Communist Party apparatus.

title: Oil math: How UAE's exit from OPEC will impact world’s energy market
content: The United Arab Emirates' exit from OPEC has significantly impacted the group's global crude oil output, reducing its share from 35% to 31%. This move, effective May 1, 2026, highlights the UAE's substantial role in the bloc. Meanwhile, regional disruptions due to the Strait of Hormuz closure are escalating, with Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait facing major production shut-ins.

title: Watch: How Argentine talisman Lionel Messi celebrated his 39th birthday
content: Lionel Messi, celebrating his 39th birthday, defied expectations by hitting the gym for an intense workout instead of taking a break. The football icon's dedication was evident in a viral Instagram video showcasing his commitment to peak performance.  Messi's continued brilliance is central to Argentina's success in the FIFA World Cup 2026, where he has already broken scoring records.

title: Israel's actions against Gaza children amount to genocidal acts, UN says
content: A UN commission report alleges Israeli forces are committing genocide in Gaza, citing the deliberate targeting of children and "unprecedented death, injury and trauma." The report highlights over 250 child fatalities since October and a systematic pattern of strikes on healthcare facilities. Israel has vehemently rejected these findings, calling the report a "propaganda piece" and a "political blood libel."

title: Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah's Tanmay Vekaria's father passes away
content: Tanmay Vekaria, known for his role as Bagha in Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah, is mourning the significant loss of his father, Arvind Vekaria, who was a long-time figure in Gujarati theatre and television. This tragedy follows the loss of his mother just eight months earlier. Arvind's legacy and cameo in the beloved sitcom profoundly influenced Tanmay's artistic journey, leaving fans and colleagues heartbroken by the news.

title: Builder did not deliver promised amenities? RERA provides legal recourse for homebuyers
content: Homebuyers facing builder delays now have a powerful recourse through RERA. This law mandates project registration, transparency, and timely possession. If your builder misses deadlines, RERA offers options: claim monthly interest for the delay or opt for a full refund with interest. Gather your documents and file a complaint online or offline to protect your investment and secure your dream home.

title: Manika Batra threatens legal action, seeks answers for Asian Games snub
content: Indian paddler Manika Batra is threatening legal action against the Table Tennis Federation of India (TTFI) over her exclusion from the Asian Games squad. Batra insists she is not seeking special selection but demands a clear, factual explanation for the decision, citing a lack of transparency. She has appealed to the Prime Minister and Sports Minister for intervention, emphasizing her desire for accountability in the selection process.

title: Ketan Agarwal case: When women become accused in the dark world of relationship crimes
content: Disturbing crime cases in India reveal how relationships built on trust can allegedly turn into deadly betrayals. Investigations into incidents like the Lohagad Fort murder and the Meghalaya honeymoon killing point towards secret relationships and conspiracies. Victims were reportedly targeted by those they knew and trusted, raising questions about the darker side of personal disputes.

title: 'Putin already compromised': Lavrov says Russia won't bend again on Ukraine, cites Trump talks
content: According to Lavrov, Putin agreed to proposals put forward by the United States during the Alaska meeting and reached an understanding with Trump on how the fighting in Ukraine could be brought to an end and how broader discussions could proceed afterwards. Lavrov said Russia remained committed to those agreements and would not support alternative interim arrangements or bend to ultimatums.

title: Kolkata warehouse collapse: 3 dead, many still trapped; top developments
content: A frantic rescue operation is underway in West Kolkata after an under-construction warehouse collapsed, trapping over 20 workers. Cranes, bulldozers, and specialized teams are working tirelessly to free those buried under the debris. Eleven injured have been rescued and hospitalized. Authorities are deploying advanced equipment, including sniffer dogs and drones, to locate survivors amidst the wreckage.

title: Selection row and disciplinary issues: Pakistan's T20 WC campaign in crisis
content: Pakistan's Women's T20 World Cup campaign is marred by internal strife, with reports of player-management friction and selection disputes. Following a string of losses, captain Fatima Sana admitted the team played its 'worst cricket.'  Allegations include disagreements over player selection and disciplinary issues, casting a shadow over their performance despite positive pre-tournament preparations.

title: Sir Alex Ferguson's favourite general robs England of its roar
content: On Day 13, Carlos Queiroz's Ghana poured cold water on England's optimism, Ronaldo broke his duck against minnows Uzbekistan and Croatia turned back the clock. Read this and more in today's Offside.

title: Within one year, “With People” resolved 210 complaints in the district
content: The programme seeks to make government services more accessible by taking the administration directly to village offices, enabling citizens to interact with officials without travelling long distances to district or taluk headquarters

title: Five dead after under-construction warehouse collapses at Taratala in Kolkata
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that the building plan of warehouse was faulty and ordered a halt on all construction activities under Kolkata Municipal Corporation area till July 31.

title: Bengaluru’s Purple Line snag raises questions over metro disruptions and crisis management
content: The technical snag on the Purple Line exposes communication gaps and coordination issues as Bengaluru’s metro network grapples with recurring disruptions

title: Iran invites Modi for official burial ceremonies of Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei
content: MEA officials did not clarify whether PM will travel for the funeral which will be held between July 3 and 9; Iran’s Petroleum Minister arrives in New Delhi to discuss investment opportunities

title: Watch: Will the sacrilege controversy cost AAP’s Bhagwant Mann his CM seat? | Above the Fold | 24.06.2026
content: In today’s episode: the row over Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann’s alleged sacrilege video, Kolkata warehouse roof collapse that left at least three people dead, criticism over Maharashtra’s RTI rules amendments, findings from a UN inquiry report on Gaza, and more.

title: UDF government’s stance on PM SHRI unconstitutional
content: The MoU was entered into by the Secretary, Education Department, Government of Kerala, and the Additional Secretary, Department of Education, Government of India, and not in the name of the President and the Governor as mandated under Article 299. Hence, the said MoU is a nullity

title: Thrikkakara municipality installing fencing along road after fatal fall of girl triggers protests
content: Twelve-year-old Rihana Kathrine Andrews, a Class VII student, succumbed to severe head injuries after losing control of her bicycle and falling from the elevated road at Mavelipuram in Kakkanad into the premises of an apartment complex below

title: A.P. to strengthen care for rare disease patients, says Health Minister
content: State plans Centre of Excellence in Vijayawada, referral centres in three regions and universal neonatal screening as part of efforts to implement the National Policy for Rare Diseases

title: No learner should be left behind, say education experts
content: Roundtable discusses roadmap for strengthening inclusive education and ensuring equal participation of students with disabilities in mainstream learning institutions across Andhra Pradesh

title: Telangana Health department issues monsoon advisory, warns of seasonal disease surge
content: Prevailing weather conditions, marked by lower temperatures and increased humidity, could lead to a rise in seasonal illnesses, particularly vector-borne, waterborne and airborne diseases, say officials

title: CJP Jantar Mantar sit-in day 5: Dipke appears before MeitY panel over blocking of X account
content: CJP’s Abhijeet Dipke questioned why similar confidentiality was not maintained in the NEET paper leak case. “Everything is confidential; you should have kept the NEET paper confidential as well. Why did that leak? These suicides would not have happened,” he said

title: Amaravati Eye set to become India’s tallest observation wheel
content: 139-metre riverfront attraction, planned under a PPP model, is expected to surpass the London Eye and emerge as a landmark tourism destination for the capital city

title: CRPF officer suspended, 20 others transferred after their families protested against CAPF Bill
content: The DIG-rank officer was suspended due to accusations that he disseminated “politically provocative” messages against the government; others transferred after their families took part in Jantar Mantar, Rajghat protests

title: ‘Operation Toofan’: over 400 arrested in Kozhikode this month
content: State-wide anti-drug campaign combines enforcement, awareness and rehabilitation efforts; 417 cases were registered and 422 people arrested in Kozhikode during the first 22 days of June

title: Watch: Mamata loyalists, rebels submit list of new office-bearers to ECI
content: Mamata Banerjee’s camp has announced a new list of party office-bearers and submitted it to the Election Commission amid a growing leadership battle within the Trinamool Congress

title: Watch: Workers dead after warehouse collapses in Kolkata
content: At least three people have died and 18 workers remain trapped after an under-construction warehouse collapsed in Kolkata’s Taratala area on June 24

title: Calicut varsity to act against self-financing college principals without UGC qualifications
content: Failure to comply with the instructions or continued deployment of unqualified principals will be viewed as a deliberate violation of university norms, says Registrar

title: No privatisation in critical minerals corridor project: Chief Minister
content: Satheesan says the project will be implemented through a consortium consisting of Coal India Ltd, Kerala Minerals and Metals Ltd., Travancore Titanium Products, and the Non-Ferrous Materials Technology Development Centre

title: Jitendra Singh pitches for BRICS Space Economy
content: Union Minister of State for Science and Technology Jitendra Singh calls for collective action among BRICS member-nations to unlock new opportunities in innovation, investment, entrepreneurship and sustainable development

title: Kerala Chief Minister promises steps to make NoRKs’ pension distribution effective
content: He says the government will examine if Life certificate submission for non-resident Keralites’ pension could be changed from annual to once every two or three years

title: Urban Andhra-2030 charts roadmap for sustainable cities
content: Government unveils urban planning reforms, digital governance initiatives and future-ready infrastructure strategy; experts stress sustainability and climate resilience as key to Andhra Pradesh’s urban growth

title: Citizens May Have To Foot Rs 1,300 Crore Bengaluru Flyover Bill
content: The DPR, prepared for Bengaluru Smart Infrastructure Limited (B-SMILE), proposes an 11.625-km corridor via Indiranagar, Domlur, Koramangala and Madiwala.

title: Air India Delhi-Amritsar Flight Briefly Enters Pak Airspace, Probe On
content: The incident has been reported to the regulatory authorities, the airline said, adding that the matter is being investigated.

title: 5 Killed, Many Feared Trapped As Warehouse Roof Collapses In Kolkata
content: Several workers were engaged in construction work at the warehouse when the roof of the three-floor building collapsed.

title: Kolkata Warehouse Roof Collapse Updates: 5 Dead, Rescue Operations Underway
content: Kolkata Warehouse Collapse Updates: Several workers were engaged in construction work at the warehouse when the roof of the three-floor building collapsed.

title: Analysis: Why Same Areas In Mumbai Get Submerged Every Monsoon
content: The issue has triggered questions as to why some parts of the city continue to flood year after year despite decades of studies, infrastructure projects, desilting drives and monsoon preparedness plans.

title: London Court Orders Nirav Modi To Pay Bank of India Over $11.5 Million
content: In a judgment handed down at the London Circuit Commercial Court on Tuesday, Justice Simon Tinkler found in favour of the Bank of India after a lengthy legal process complicated by paperwork transfer delays within the UK prison service.

title: Iran Invites PM Modi To Former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's Funeral
content: The invitation marks a significant diplomatic engagement following the death of the 86-year-old leader, who was killed in a joint US-Israeli airstrike on February 28.

title: Piyush Goyal To Visit UK To Discuss Trade Pact Implementation
content: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal will undertake an official visit to London from June 25-27 for the discussions, an official statement said on Wednesday.

title: Centre Notifies New Telecom Rules, Ending Decades-Old Licensing System
content: The Centre has also notified the Telecom eServices Portal' that enables the digital implementation of the provisions of the Telecom Act.

title: Pak Man Kept French Wife Trapped For 10 Years. Son Gets Cops To Free Her
content: The woman, identified as French national Sylvie Yasmina, told police that her husband was "very violent" and abused the family "on a daily basis", according to local reports.

title: Andhra Cop Arrested In Custodial Death Case, Plea For CBI Probe In Court
content: Sai Krishna's mother, Gade Vijayalakshmi, had approached the High Court alleging that her son was picked up by police near Markapuram in the first week of May and brought to Krishnalanka Police Station in Vijayawada.

title: UDF, LDF Clash Over PM SHRI, Accuse Each Other Of Yielding Ground To Centre
content: The UDF defended its position in the Kerala Assembly by reading out the actions taken by the previous LDF government.

title: Harassment Case At Spanish Firm In Pune, Chief Minister Assures Action
content: Shiv Sena member Manisha Kayande said that a female employee of a Spanish multinational operating in Pune district was sacked immediately after she filed a complaint of sexual harassment.

title: Revanth Reddy's Message To Congress Leaders As Telangana SIR Begins Tomorrow
content: Addressing ministers, MPs, MLAs and constituency in-charges during a Zoom review meeting, Revanth Reddy said the SIR exercise was a "very serious matter" amid fears that names of genuine voters could be deleted from the rolls.

title: From Drones To New Battle Formations: The Legacy Of General Upendra Dwivedi
content: General Dwivedi pursued capability-centric warfare as a successor to the platform-centric models that had long defined Indian military thinking.

title: 315 Districts Across India At High Risk Due To Rainfall Deficit, El Nino
content: Of the 315 districts, 111 districts with less than 25 per cent irrigation coverage were categorised as the most vulnerable.

title: Andhra Pradesh High Court Seeks Detailed Report On Missing Tuni Girl
content: The court specifically asked authorities to clarify whether the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) -- mandated by the Supreme Court in cases of missing children -- had been strictly followed.

title: 24x7 Control Room, Relief Camps: Delhi's New Plan To Prevent Flood
content: Apart from flood management, authorities are also focusing on one of Delhi's most persistent monsoon challenges - urban waterlogging.

title: Iran Not Exacting Strait Of Hormuz Toll: Donald Trump
content: Tehran has repeatedly said it plans to charge what it calls maritime service fees for crossing the strait.

title: A.P. Waqf Board recovers 700 acres of land, plans new initiatives
content: Addressing the 15th board meeting, Waqf Board Chairman says several encroached lands — 56 acres in Kovur, 24 acres in Kandukur, and 34 acres in Ramayapatnam — have been reclaimed in the last couple of years

title: CM is trying to cover up corruption in Gruha Lakshmi, says Tenginakai
content: The Hubballi-Dharwad Central MLA says Shivakumar is now talking of review of beneficiaries as there has been large-scale corruption

title: Hariprasad seeks to know the difference between Mahmud Ghazni and Modi-Shah ‘looting’ temple money
content: The new KPCC president questions the silence of the PM and Union Home Minister Amit Shah on the ‘misuse’ of Ram Mandir donations

title: Leaders promote charity for equitable wealth distribution in society
content: Inaugurating V. Siddarami Reddy Government Junior College building at Chinnagottigallu mandal, Tirupati district in-charge Minister explains that the government is committed on popularising charity among the affluent to benefit their immediate society

title: Pulikali funds blocked over non-compliance with Centre’s conditions: BJP Thrissur city district president
content: Failure to display Tourism Ministry and ‘Incredible India’ logos on publicity materials cited as key lapse

title: KERA Productive Alliance scheme to strengthen smallholder farmers in Kollam
content: The initiative will operate under the Productive Alliance Scheme of the Kerala Climate Resilient Agri-Value Chain Modernisation project, ensuring sustainable pricing and secure markets for local farmers

title: Indian aircraft briefly drifts into Pak airspace, probe ordered
content: the flight reportedly strayed into Pakistani airspace on Tuesday night . instead of landing at Amritsar’s Sri Guru Ramdas Ji International Airport, the flight was diverted back to Delhi due to heavy air traffic . it later departed again from Delhi and landed in Amritar at 2.10am on June 24 .
